Tests if converting a function returning Infinity to a number results in NaN.

# Purpose
This code is a single assertion statement that tests the behavior of the `Number` function when it is given a function as an argument. The `assert` function checks if the result of `Number(function a() {return Infinity})` is `NaN` (Not-a-Number). The code uses the `isNaN` function to verify that the conversion of a function to a number results in `NaN`, which is the expected behavior according to JavaScript's type conversion rules. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific test case for type conversion in JavaScript.
